rulings.Case Title: EDUARDO MENDOZA et al VS. CAFE COCOMO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 3, 2010, line 1, CROSS-DEFENDANT ABITSCH, JEFFREY, ABITSCH, GEORGE, ABITSCH & ABITSCH, LLC, A LIMITED LIABILTIY DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT. DEMURRER OVERRULED AS TO LLC AND THE INDIVIDUALS. THE FACT THAT THE INJURY OCCURRED OFF THE PREMISES IS NOT DISPOSITIVE AND WOULD BE MORE APPROPRIATELY ADDRESSED IN A MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. SEE BARNES V. BLACK (1999) 71 CAL.APP.4TH 1473. THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT, WHICH IS INCORPORATED INTO THE CROSS-COMPLAINT, ALLEGES THAT THE INDIVIDUALS AS WELL AS THE LLC OWN THE PROPERTY. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICK HELLAND VS. RAY BRONNER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR IMPOSITION OF SANCTIONS
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 3, 2010, line 2, DEFENDANT RAY BRONNER'S MOTION FOR IMPOSITION OF SANCTIONS. MOTION FOR IMPOSITION OF SANCTIONS IS DENIED. THE MOTION IS PROCEDURALLY AND SUBSTANTIVELY DEFECTIVE. DEFENDANT DID NOT COMPLY WITH THE SAFE HARBOR PROVISION SET FORTH IN CCP SECTION 128.7(c)(1). DEFENDANT FAILS TO SHOW THAT THIS ACTION IS "FRIVOLOUS" WITHIN THE MEANING OF CCP SECTION 128.7. PLAINTIFFS REQUEST FOR ATTORNEY FEES IS DENIED. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: NEWPORT BUILDERS INC, A NEVADA CORPORATION et al VS. NORTH BAY CONSTRUCTION, INC, A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 3, 2010, line 5, DEFENDANT DON RAMATICI INSURANCE, INC.'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. AS TO THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION (FRAUD), PLAINTIFFS MUST PLEAD: (1)HOW DRI MISREPRESENTED ITS AUTHORITY; (2)HOW PLAINTIFFS (NOT FVI) HAVE SUFFERED HARM; AND (3)HOW THE MISREPRESENTATION WAS AUTHORIZED AND/OR RATIFIED BY DRI. AS TO THE 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION (NEGLIGENCE) PLAINTIFFS MUST PLEAD: (1)WHEN PLAINTIFFS HIRED COUNSEL AND BEGAN INCURRING FEES ON THE UNDERLYING LAWSUIT; (2)HOW/WHY A DUTY WAS OWED TO PLAINTIFFS; AND (3)WHETHER THEY ARE ASSERTING A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE AND/OR NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION. AS TO THE 8TH CAUSE OF ACTION (BREACH OF 3RD PARTY BENEFICIARY CONTRACT), PLAINTIFFS MUST PLEAD WHETHER THE CONTRACT WAS WRITTEN OR ORAL. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: UNITED BRANDS PRODUCTS DESIGN, DEVELOPMENT AND VS. MOSA INDUSTRIAL CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 3, 2010, line 9, CROSS-DEFENDANT UNITED BRANDS PRODUCTS DESIGN, DEVELOPMENT AND DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT. DEMURRER OVERRULED AS TO 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION (FRAUD & DECEIT - CONCEALMENT). DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION (FRAUD & DECEIT - PROMISE WITHOUT INTENT TO PERFORM). ALTHOUGH THE APPLICATION OF THE LITIGATION PRIVILEGE CANNOT BE RESOLVED ON THIS DEMURRER, CROSS-COMPLAINANT MUST ALLEGE IN WHAT CAPACITY THOSE SENDING EMAILS WERE ACTING AND TO WHOM THE EMAILS WERE SENT. =(302/CWW)
